intro.title: THREE COMMANDS. ZERO CONFIG.
intro.description: Zenith’s stable path stays literal: scaffold through create-zenith, step into the generated app, and start the dev loop. Open each command for the exact contract behind it.

stablePath.label: Stable Path
stablePath.beforeCode: The canonical stable scaffold entry is
stablePath.afterCode: . Beta installs stay opt-in, and the full compatibility proof still lives in the install contract docs.

panelBody.afterCode: is the canonical stable scaffold entry. It generates the project, applies optional tooling only when requested, and exits without becoming part of runtime or build orchestration.

steps.heading: Enter the generated project
steps.panelBody: The scaffolded app already reflects the choices made during setup. If you declined ESLint, Prettier, or path aliases, Zenith does not leave behind placeholder scripts or stray config files.
steps.asideBody: Same version plus the same scaffold answers must produce the same file tree deterministically.

steps.heading: Start the dev loop

panelBody.beforeCode: This is the canonical local loop used across Zenith starters. For full compatibility verification, the install contract also checks a clean-room install and

steps.asideBody: Stable and train releases are validated when scaffold, install, and build succeed deterministically.

followThrough.label: Canonical follow-through
followThrough.note: The section above is the quick local start. The install compatibility contract extends that path into a deterministic install-and-build proof for stable releases.
